display.purpose: Hero headline only — Source Serif 4, the authority of print.

lede.purpose: Serif subhead. Literate, calm, carries the consistency promise.

label.purpose: JetBrains Mono — the machine's voice only (code, diagnostics, CLI, labels).

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Transient overlapping UI only (dropdown, popover, dialog). Never on a card or button at rest.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions and entrances. Exponential decel, no overshoot.

motion.name: dur-move
motion.purpose: Cursor travel, reveals, larger position changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The high-commitment action. Solid ink fill, mono uppercase label, square.

components.name: Line Button
components.description: Secondary action. Hairline border, no fill, no shadow.

components.name: Install Chip
components.description: The inline CLI command with a copy affordance. The most-clicked element; plain by design.
components.html: $ pnpm create kovo my-app

components.name: Link
components.description: Inline prose link. Indigo with a soft underline that solidifies on hover.

components.name: Verdict Callout
components.description: Status callout with a leading filled chip carrying the color (never a left stripe). Shown in the stale state.
components.html: ✕Header shows 2, cart shows 3 — the stale UI most frameworks ship.

components.name: Code / Terminal Frame
components.description: Dark frame for code and captured toolchain output, in both themes. Reads as evidence.
components.html:
server/queries/product.tskovo check
✗ KV251 cart/add updates items, but the header reads cart.count
  → add cart.count to the touch set

components.name: Section Heading
components.description: Serif section title. The display family carries authority; body stays Inter.

narrative.overview: Kovo's thesis is that an application's behavior should be provable, checked end to end before anything runs. The site makes that thesis visible by presenting itself as a typeset proof: the restraint of a well-set engineering paper, the precision of a reference manual, the confidence of an author who shows the failure cases. The system is editorial-technical — a near-neutral off-white ground, a single deep-indigo accent used like an errata mark, serif display over a grotesque body, with monospace reserved for the machine's voice and dark terminal frames that read as evidence.
keyCharacteristics: Off-white neutral ground (chroma 0), never cream or warm-tinted.
One indigo accent, used on ≤10% of any screen.
Serif display + grotesque body + mono for machine voice.
Square frames, hairline borders, flat by default. No decorative shadows.
Dark code/terminal frames in both themes — evidence, not chrome.
Red means stale/error, green means in-sync; semantics, never the brand color.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Indigo appears on no more than ~10% of any screen. Its rarity is what makes it read as a deliberate mark rather than decoration.

rules.name: The Semantics-Aren't-Branding Rule
rules.body: Red is always stale/error and green is always in-sync. Never recolor a status to the brand, and never use indigo to mean good.

rules.name: The Machine-Voice Rule
rules.body: Monospace is reserved for things the machine produced or consumes: code, diagnostics, file paths, CLI commands, key labels. Never set prose in mono to look technical.

rules.name: The Display-Serif-Only Rule
rules.body: The serif is for display, headline, and lede. Body prose is always the grotesque. Two competing serifs at reading size is forbidden.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat. Use a hairline border or a tonal step instead of a shadow. A 1px border plus a wide soft shadow on one element (the ghost-card) is forbidden.

dos: Do keep the ground a true neutral off-white (#fafafa, chroma 0). Carry warmth in type and accent, never the body background.
Do use Indigo on ≤10% of any screen.
Do set headings in the serif and body in Inter; reserve mono for the machine's voice.
Do draw structure with 1px hairline borders and tonal steps.
Do keep red = stale/error and green = in-sync, everywhere.
Do pair every animation with a prefers-reduced-motion fallback to the consistent state.
Do verify body text ≥4.5:1 and large text ≥3:1 in both themes, including tinted callouts.
donts: Don't ship the SaaS-cream dev-tool look: gradient-purple hero, rounded glassy cards, tracked-uppercase eyebrow on every section, identical icon-card grid.
Don't make hype claims without a runnable, reproducible artifact beside them.
Don't over-animate: no scroll-jacking, no parallax-everything, no decorative motion.
Don't go sterile either — default-browser styling with no point of view is the opposite failure.
Don't use a colored border-left/right greater than 1px as an accent stripe. Use the leading status chip.
Don't pair a 1px border with a wide box-shadow (≥16px blur) on the same element.
Don't round cards to 24px+. Cards are square or 4-6px; pills are for tags only.
Don't recolor a status to the brand, or use Indigo to mean good.
